news.title: Basic Materials Roundup: Market Talk
news.summary: Front-month gold losing 13.4% to $4,022.90 a troy ounce in the quarter, the worst since 2Q 2013, with most of the losses coming in June.  Silver drops 20.4% to $59.477 a troy ounce in the quarter, its worst since 1Q 2020, and the largest monthly drop since September 2011.  Precious metals have been pressured by a stronger dollar and indications of rising interest rates in coming months, says Tony Sage of Critical Metals in a note.

news.title: Michael Burry bets against Tesla, Nvidia in new AI bubble short positions

news.title: 'The jury's still out' on AI winners and losers, investor says
news.summary:
STORY: "This market believes in AI one day and then it's just scared about the spending. Now all these companies that have pristine balance sheets, not only are they issuing stock, but they're also taking on a lot of debt. They are making huge bets," said Diton.

He said the scale of the spending has made it harder to determine which companies will ultimately benefit.

"Everyone may not win. There could be some winners, there could be some losers. Look at Microsoft stock. It has come down a lot from the high. So, the jury's still out in terms of who wins and who loses."

news.title: SpaceX and Tesla Shares Are Trading Like Twins
news.summary: SpaceX  stock and  Tesla  shares are doing something surprising: They are rising and falling together.  Shares of Elon Musk’s rocket and AI company gained 4.1%, closing at $170.86 on Tuesday, while the  and  were up 0.8% and 0.3%, respectively.  SpaceX stock gained 7.2%, its best day since June 15, which was the second day of trading post-IPO.
